name: Ceci 2015 Tadalafil Exercise-Induced Damage Extract
population_or_context: Healthy males undergoing exhaustive exercise after tadalafil or placebo.
sample_size: Healthy male crossover sample; abstract does not state the sample size.
methods: Double-blind crossover trial measuring oxidative status, muscle damage markers, IL-6, and antioxidant capacity before and after exhaustive exercise.
population_profile: Healthy males; directly relevant to recovery-risk hypotheses but not a performance trial.
tadalafil_exposure: Two tadalafil 20 mg/day doses separated by 36 hours before exhaustive exercise.
indication_context: Experimental exercise recovery and oxidative-stress physiology.
safety_findings: Tadalafil decreased antioxidant capacity at rest and increased exercise-induced oxidative and muscle-damage marker responses versus placebo.
directness_to_target_population: Moderate for acute exhaustive exercise biomarkers in healthy males, low for clinical injury, delayed-onset soreness, adaptation, or chronic low-dose users.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Biomarker endpoints, small/unstated sample size in abstract, short time horizon, and no clinical performance or injury outcomes.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence, but high acute exposure and biomarker outcomes limit transfer to daily 5 mg gym use or clinical recovery claims.
